heroKicker: I left my 9-5 job to pursue my passion for cooking
heroSub: I recently graduated from the Institute of Culinary Education in New York City. I believe that food connects us, and I am passionate about sharing the simple, real food I make at home so you can make it too. Come cook with me!
aboutText:
About a year ago, I was sitting at a corporate desk job, wondering what I was doing with my life. After many long days of questioning everything and a few tears shed, I made what felt like a crazy decision to quit my job and go to culinary school in New York City.

Each day at culinary school, my passion for cooking grew. Even more importantly, I had a new sense of joy in my daily life -- I was finally spending my time doing something I genuinely loved. I'd be lying if I said there still weren't hard days and difficult moments. Learning how to cook is a messy process filled with mistakes, burns, and the occasional painful cut. However, even in the times where I struggled, I still knew that I was where I was meant to be, doing something I loved.

This is home base now: the recipes I come back to, the things I'm still learning, and whatever I'm cooking lately. Pull up a chair.

This website is the culmination of what I learned in culinary school, the recipes I come back to again and again, and new techniques I am trying out. I am so excited you are here!

recipes.title: Smash-Style Burgers (No Burger Press Needed)
recipes.blurb: These burgers are SO GOOD. They are thin patties that get an amazing crust, topped with American cheese, caramelized onion, and my not-so secret special sauce.
recipes.ingredientsText:
1 package ground beef
Salt
Pepper
Garlic Powder
1 White Onion
American Cheese
Pickles
Mayonaise
Ketchup
Mustard
recipes.methodText:
Prep the Patties:
Divide the ground beef into 3-ounce portions and gently roll each into a ball.
Place each ball between two sheets of parchment paper (or cover the top with one sheet of parchment and place on a cutting board) and use the bottom of a heavy pot or pan to press it as thin as possible. The thinner the patty, the more you'll get that classic smash burger texture and crispy edges.
Transfer the patties to a sheet pan lined with parchment and refrigerate while you prepare the toppings.
Make the Sauce & Prep the Toppings:
Finely dice the onion and set aside.
Separate enough slices of American cheese for each patty.
Finely dice the pickles and add them to a small bowl.
Stir in the ketchup, mustard, mayonnaise, pickle juice, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until smooth and set aside.
Butter your burger buns.
Heat a cast-iron skillet (or any large skillet) over medium heat. Add a thin layer of neutral oil (Ex., canola or vegetable oil) and let it heat until shimmering.
Place a patty in the pan and cook for about 2 minutes. Since these patties are extremely thin, they'll cook through very quickly.
While the burger cooks, add the diced onions to the side of the pan and cook them in the beef fat until soft and translucent.
Flip the patty, top with a slice of American cheese, and cook for another 1–2 minutes, just until the cheese is melted.
While the cheese melts, place the buttered buns cut-side down in the pan and toast until golden brown.
Assemble:
Spread the burger sauce on both buns. Add 2–3 patties (they're intentionally thin), top with the cooked onions, and finish with any additional toppings you like, such as lettuce, tomato, or extra pickles. Serve immediately.

recipes.title: Spicy Calabrian Chilli Vodka Sauce
recipes.blurb: Classic vodka sauce -- but better. Spicy, creamy, and satiating.
recipes.ingredientsText:
1 lb pasta of your choice
2 tbsp olive oil
1 medium shallot, finely minced
2 cloves garlic, minced
2 tbsp tomato paste
1–2 tbsp Calabrian chili oil (adjust to your spice preference)
¼ cup vodka (optional)
1 cup heavy cream
½–¾ cup reserved pasta water, as needed
2 tbsp cold unsalted butter
Salt, to taste
Black pepper, to taste
Mozzarella balls for garnish (optional)
Fresh basil for garnish (optional)
recipes.methodText:
Heat the olive oil in a large sauté pan over medium heat until it ripples.
Add the shallot and cook for 3–4 minutes, until softened and translucent. Stir in the garlic and cook for 30–60 seconds, just until fragrant. Be careful not to let the garlic brown.
Add the tomato paste and cook for 2–3 minutes, stirring frequently, until it deepens in color and becomes slightly caramelized.
Stir in the Calabrian chili oil until well combined.
If using, pour in the vodka and cook for 2–3 minutes, allowing it to reduce and the alcohol to cook off.
Lower the heat and stir in the heavy cream until the sauce is smooth. Add 1/4 cup reserved pasta water, then add more as needed until the sauce reaches your desired consistency.
Turn off the heat and whisk in the cold butter until melted and emulsified.
Add the cooked pasta and toss until evenly coated. If needed, add another splash of pasta water to help the sauce cling to the pasta.
Taste and adjust with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper before serving.

recipes.title: Steak & Potatoes
recipes.blurb: Good steak really doesn't need much. A hot pan, salt, and a simple pan sauce bursting with flavor. School approved.
recipes.ingredientsText:
For the Steak
1 strip steak (10–12 oz)
Canola oil
2 tbsp butter
2 garlic cloves, smashed
2–3 sprigs thyme or rosemary (optional)
Salt
Black pepper
For the Potatoes
2 russet potatoes, medium dice
Canola oil
2 garlic cloves, minced
Salt
Black pepper
Parsley for garnish 
For the Glazed Carrots
1 bunch baby carrots with tops attached, peeled
2 tbsp butter
1 tsp sugar
Salt
For the Red Wine Pan Sauce
1 shallot, finely minced
½ cup red wine
1 cup chicken stock
2 tbsp cold butter, cubed
Parsley, for garnish
recipes.methodText:
Steak:
Season steak generously with salt and pepper. Let sit at room temperature while you prep everything else.
Potatoes:
Medium-dice potatoes. If not cooking immediately, keep in cold water to prevent browning. Drain and dry well before cooking.
Heat canola oil in a medium pan (enough to coat the bottom generously). Use a pan large enough so potatoes aren’t crowded. Once oil is hot, add potatoes in a single layer. Let them sit initially, then toss occasionally so all sides crisp evenly. Cook until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove and set aside on a paper towel or rack.
Carrots:
Blanch carrots in salted water for ~4 minutes. Transfer immediately to an ice bath to stop cooking.
When ready to serve, heat butter in a small pan. Add carrots and sugar. Toss until evenly glazed and glossy. 
Steak Part 2:
Heat a small amount of canola oil in a cast iron or heavy pan until shimmering. Sear steak fat-side down first until rendered and browned. Lay steak flat and sear until deep color develops.
Flip steak and lower heat. Add butter, garlic, and thyme or rosemary if using. Baste continuously.
Cook to desired doneness, then remove steak and let rest DO NOT cut immediately. NOTE: steak will continue to cook once removed from pan due to carry over heat, so pull steak off pan a couple degrees lower than your desired temperature. 
Pan Sauce:
Keep pan as-is (do not clean). Add shallots to residual fat and cook ~1 minute. Turn off heat and pour in red wine. Note: this may cause a flambe reaction, so remove pan from heat before adding wine. Return to heat and reduce wine by ~70%.
Add chicken stock and reduce by ~50–60% until lightly nappé consistency.
Turn off heat. Whisk in cold butter until glossy. Finish with parsley.
Plating:
Rewarm carrots if needed. Slice steak against the grain. Plate steak with sauce, potatoes, and carrots arranged cleanly.

recipes.blurb: Light, fresh, and vegetable forward. Proof that simple ingredients at their peak beat fussy every single time.

recipes.blurb: Eggs, sauces, knife work. The little techniques from school that quietly make everything else you cook better.

workIntro: I team up with brands I actually use and genuinely want to share with others. If you have a product you think would be a good fit, please reach out!
workOfferingsText:
Recipe development
Sponsored recipes & reels
Short-form video (IG + TikTok)
Product features & reviews
Brand partnerships
UGC-style content
